title: Advanced SQLite Memory Session
description: Storing and retrieving conversation items with branch/turn tracking in a SQLite-backed memory session
class_hierarchy: AdvancedSQLiteSession imports: RunResult, Usage, TResponseInputItem, SessionSettings
initialization_flow: _init_structure_tables() → SQLiteDB: CREATE TABLE message_structure (id, session_id, message_id, branch_id, message_type, sequence_number, user_turn_number, branch_turn_number, tool_name, created_at)
_init_structure_tables() → SQLiteDB: CREATE TABLE turn_usage (id, session_id, branch_id, user_turn_number, requests, input/output/total_tokens, input_tokens_details JSON, output_tokens_details JSON)
_init_structure_tables() → SQLiteDB: CREATE 5 indexes on message_structure and turn_usage
add_items_flow: _insert_structure_metadata() → SELECT last N ids FROM agent_messages ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT len(items)
_insert_structure_metadata() → SELECT MAX(sequence_number) FROM message_structure WHERE session_id
_insert_structure_metadata() → SELECT MAX(user_turn_number), MAX(branch_turn_number) FROM message_structure WHERE session_id AND branch_id
for each item: _extract_tool_name(item) → tool name string or None
for each item: _is_user_message(item) → increments user_message_count, updates item_turn/item_branch_turn
_insert_structure_metadata() → INSERT INTO message_structure executemany(session_id, message_id, branch_id, message_type, sequence_number, user_turn_number, branch_turn_number, tool_name)
on structure metadata error: _cleanup_orphaned_messages_sync(conn) → DELETE orphaned agent_messages rows
get_items_flow: _get_items_sync() → SELECT m.message_data FROM agent_messages m JOIN message_structure s ON m.id = s.message_id WHERE session_id=? AND branch_id=? ORDER BY sequence_number ASC [LIMIT ?]
if limit applied: rows reversed to maintain chronological order
for each row: json.loads(message_data) → item dict
get_next_turn_number_flow: SELECT COALESCE(MAX(user_turn_number), 0) FROM message_structure WHERE session_id=? AND branch_id=?
store_run_usage_flow: store_run_usage() checks result.context_wrapper.usage is not None

_update_turn_usage_internal → INSERT OR REPLACE INTO turn_usage (session_id, branch_id, user_turn_number, requests, input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens, input_tokens_details, output_tokens_details)
branching_flow: _validate_turn() → SELECT message_data FROM message_structure JOIN agent_messages WHERE branch_turn_number=? AND message_type='user'
_copy_messages_to_new_branch() → SELECT ms.* WHERE branch_turn_number < from_turn_number ORDER BY sequence_number
_copy_messages_to_new_branch() → INSERT INTO message_structure (new rows with new_branch_id, new sequence_numbers, same message_ids)
switch_to_branch() → asyncio.to_thread(_validate_branch) → SELECT COUNT(*) FROM message_structure WHERE branch_id=?
delete_branch() guards: cannot delete 'main'; if deleting current and force=True → switch_to_branch('main') first
delete_branch() → DELETE FROM turn_usage WHERE branch_id=?
delete_branch() → DELETE FROM message_structure WHERE branch_id=?
key_methods: add_items(items) -> None  [async, inserts messages + structure metadata atomically]

db_tables.agent_sessions: inherited from SQLiteSession — stores session records
db_tables.agent_messages: inherited from SQLiteSession — stores message_data JSON blobs
db_tables.message_structure: branch_id, sequence_number, user_turn_number, branch_turn_number, message_type, tool_name — links messages to branches/turns
db_tables.turn_usage: branch_id, user_turn_number, requests, input/output/total_tokens, JSON details — per-turn token analytics

key_relationships: Branch isolation: same physical message rows, different message_structure entries per branch
asyncio.to_thread wraps all sync SQLite calls for async-safe operation
important_notes: State: _current_branch_id (default 'main') tracks active branch in memory
Branch copy shares actual message_data rows — only message_structure rows are duplicated
store_run_usage() must be called manually after each Runner.run() — not automatic
Atomicity: add_items() does _insert_items + _insert_structure_metadata in one _locked_connection critical section; on metadata failure, orphaned messages are cleaned up
Session usage is aggregated on demand from turn_usage (not stored separately)
Tool name extraction handles: MCP tools (server_label.name), built-in types (computer_call, file_search_call, etc.), function tools with 'name' field (qualified with namespace)
